_source: Gen 2 Compendiums/MEASUREMENT.pdf (Set 1 Measurement, Set 2 Scale), TASC MTG215123 Module 3 Topic 2. Answer keys verified by tools/verify_answers.py (pi from math library).

unit.title: Shape & Measurement
unit.subtitle: Module 3 · Topic 2
unit.description: Mensuration and scale: perimeter, circumference and area of plane shapes and composites; volume and surface area of standard solids; and ratio, similarity and scale factors, including the effect of scale on area and volume. Criteria C8 and C1.

lessons.title: Perimeter, circumference & area
lessons.overview: Measuring around and inside plane shapes.

sections.title: Perimeter and circumference
sections.html: The perimeter is the distance around a shape — add the side lengths. For a circle this is the circumference: C = 2πr (or πd). A circle of radius 5 has circumference 2π×5 ≈ 31.42. An arc is a fraction of the circumference.

sections.title: Area
sections.html:
Common areas:

  • Rectangle: length × width.
  • Triangle: ½ × base × height.
  • Circle: πr². A circle of radius 7 has area π×49 ≈ 153.94.
  • A sector is a fraction of a circle's area.

Composite shapes are broken into standard pieces, whose areas are then added or subtracted.

sections.title: Volume
sections.html:
Volume is the space inside a solid:

  • Rectangular prism (cuboid): L × w × h.
  • Any prism: cross-sectional area × length (A × h).
  • Cylinder: πr²h.
  • Cone: ⅓πr²h  (a tapered solid is ⅓ of the prism it fits inside).
  • Pyramid: ⅓ × base area × height.
  • Sphere: &frac43;πr³.

sections.title: Surface area
sections.html: The total surface area is the sum of the areas of every face. Cuboid: 2Lw + 2Lh + 2wh. Sphere: 4πr². Cone: πrs + πr² (s = slant height). For composites, add the exposed faces.

sections.title: Ratio and scale
sections.html:
A ratio compares quantities. To divide a quantity in a ratio, share it into the total number of parts. Dividing $60 in the ratio 2:3 gives parts of 60÷5 = $12, so the shares are $24 and $36.

A map scale such as 1:50 000 means 1 unit on the map is 50 000 units in reality. A 4 cm map distance represents 4×50 000 = 200 000 cm (= 2 km).

sections.title: Similarity and the effect of scale
sections.html:
Similar figures have the same shape; corresponding lengths share a common scale factor k. Crucially:

  • Lengths scale by k.
  • Areas scale by k².
  • Volumes scale by k³.

So doubling every length (k = 2) multiplies area by 4 and volume by 8.

quizzes.title: Quiz: Perimeter & area
quizzes.overview: Circumference and areas of plane shapes. Use π = 3.14159. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: Find the circumference of a circle with radius 5 cm. (π ≈ 3.14159)
Find the area of a circle with radius 7 cm. (π ≈ 3.14159)
Find the area of a rectangle 8 cm by 5 cm.
Find the perimeter of a rectangle 8 cm by 5 cm.
Find the area of a triangle with base 10 cm and height 6 cm.
questions.explain: Area = ½ × base × height = ½ × 10 × 6 = 30 cm².

questions.prompt: The area of a circle is given by which formula?
questions.explain: Area of a circle is πr². 2πr and πd are the circumference; 4πr² is the surface area of a sphere.

quizzes.title: Quiz: Volume & surface area
quizzes.overview: Volumes and surface areas of solids. Use π = 3.14159. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: Find the volume of a rectangular prism 4 cm × 3 cm × 2 cm.
Find the total surface area of a rectangular prism 4 cm × 3 cm × 2 cm.
Find the volume of a cylinder with radius 3 cm and height 10 cm. (π ≈ 3.14159)
Find the volume of a cone with radius 3 cm and height 4 cm. (π ≈ 3.14159)
Find the volume of a sphere with radius 6 cm. (π ≈ 3.14159)
Find the surface area of a sphere with radius 6 cm. (π ≈ 3.14159)
The volume of a cone is what fraction of a cylinder with the same radius and height?
questions.explain: A cone (a tapered solid) has one third of the volume of the prism/cylinder it fits inside.

quizzes.title: Quiz: Ratio, scale & similarity
quizzes.overview: Dividing in a ratio, map scales and the effect of scale on area and volume. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: Divide $60 in the ratio 2 : 3. What is the LARGER share?
questions.explain: 5 parts: 60 ÷ 5 = $12 per part; the larger share is 3 × 12 = $36.

questions.prompt: On a 1:50 000 map, a distance measures 4 cm. What real distance does this represent, in centimetres?
questions.explain: Actual = map distance × scale = 4 × 50 000 = 200 000 cm (2 km).

questions.prompt: Two similar figures have a length scale factor of 3. By what factor is the AREA scaled?
questions.explain: Area scales by the square of the length scale factor: 3² = 9.

questions.prompt: Two similar solids have a length scale factor of 2. By what factor is the VOLUME scaled?
questions.explain: Volume scales by the cube of the length scale factor: 2³ = 8.

questions.prompt: If every length of a shape is enlarged by a scale factor of 4, its area is enlarged by a factor of:
